articles.title: Taiwan's Lai lands in Eswatini in a trip delayed by lack of overflight clearance
articles.summary: Eswatini remains the only African nation without tariff-free access to China's market due to its ties with Taiwan.

articles.title: Trump’s Vision for D.C. ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statues Grows in Size and Cost
articles.summary: Plans for the project include formal gardens, reflecting pools and plazas alongside the statues of 250 notable Americans, according to documents obtained by The New York Times.

articles.title: Timmy the stranded whale rescued after weekslong effort
articles.summary: Timmy captured the hearts of whale lovers across the globe who rooted for a happy ending for the humpback.

articles.title: Academy announces major overhaul to rules
articles.summary: The new rules focus on areas such as AI protections for writers and actors and expanded eligibility for international films.

articles.title: Abortion Providers Forced to Adapt After Court Ruling Blocks Pill Access by Mail
articles.summary: The Fifth Circuit court’s ruling, which is being appealed, reinstates a requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone, upending abortion access in the United

articles.title: What to Know About the Mideast Standoff
articles.summary: Negotiations to end the war are at an impasse over Iran’s nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz, which remains mostly shut.

articles.title: Supreme Court Asked to Restore Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
articles.summary: A federal appeals court temporarily halted a Food and Drug Administration regulation that has greatly expanded access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

articles.title: Banksy confirms new statue installed in central London is his work
articles.summary: The statue in Waterloo Place, which appeared early Wednesday, depicts a man proudly hoisting a flag— but the flag is blinding him.

articles.title: U.S. Fast-Tracks Arms Deals Valued at $8.6 Billion to Mideast Partners
articles.summary: The Persian Gulf countries and Israel have faced repeated Iranian attacks during the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran. The State Department move bypassed congressional review.

articles.title: Imprisoned Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner in critical condition
articles.summary: Narges Mohammadi, an Iranian activist and 2023 Nobel Peace Prize laureate, was hospitalized after collapsing in prison. Her family says her condition has deteriorated since a March heart attack.

articles.title: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany. But Thousands Will Remain.
articles.summary: The U.S. military has tens of thousands of personnel in Germany, more than in any other foreign country but Japan. Their significance extends well beyond Europe.

articles.title: How Germany May Have Misjudged Trump’s Anger on Iran
articles.summary: Germany had appeared not to believe President Trump’s threats to pull troops from the country. Once it was announced, Berlin offered a measured response.

articles.title: Opinion: The everyday tragedy of gun violence
articles.summary: The White House Correspondents Association Dinner was one of several incidents of gun violence in the U.S. last week. Others ended in injuries and fatalities.

articles.title: Can Trump's latest pick for surgeon general make it through confirmation?
articles.summary: Nicole Saphier, a breast cancer radiologist, is the president's third nominee for surgeon general. Will she get confirmed?

articles.title: Americans aren't sleeping enough. Here's what could help
articles.summary: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.

articles.title: New Netflix documentary reexamines Winnie Mandela's divisive legacy
articles.summary: Winnie Madikizela-Mandela is one of the most revered — and controversial — women in South African history. In a new documentary her granddaughters examine the liberation icon in all her complexity.

articles.title: After Assad's fall, Syria's Kurds are left in limbo, feeling abandoned by the U.S.
articles.summary: Caught in limbo after the fall of Syria's Bashar al-Assad, Kurdish families struggle with cold, loss and uncertainty — feeling abandoned by the U.S. allies they once fought alongside.

articles.title: Trump’s Push for Electoral Retribution Heads to the Ballot Box
articles.summary: The president’s push to punish political enemies in his own party will play a starring role in a series of Republican primaries this month in which he has backed challengers.

articles.title: Air Force says former Qatari 747 will be ready to fly as Air Force One this summer
articles.summary: The U.S. Air Force has finished modifying and testing a Boeing 747 jet donated by Qatar for temporary use as Air Force One and expects to have it ready for President Trump to use this summer.

articles.title: U.S. to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany in next 6-12 months
articles.summary: The United States will withdraw about 5,000 troops from Germany, the Pentagon said Friday, fulfilling President Donald Trump's threat as he clashes with the German leader over the U.S. war with Iran.

articles.title: After Canada loosens citizenship requirements, thousands of people apply
articles.summary: After Canada changed a law opening up citizenship opportunities, thousands of Americans are trying to become citizens of their northern neighbor. It's partly for political and personal reasons.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
